npc_seeds.name: Elena Marquette, Elysium Curator
npc_seeds.summary: An ancilla Toreador who curates the city’s main Elysium, treating it as both gallery and court stage.
npc_seeds.role: Elysium curator and social power broker
npc_seeds.temperament: polished, witty, quietly ruthless when her aesthetic is challenged
possible_plots: Elena plans a themed Elysium exhibit that subtly insults a rival Primogen, and the coterie must decide whether to support, sabotage, or redirect the fallout.
She offers the PCs a place of honor in an upcoming event if they procure a specific stolen artwork for her.
A piece in her collection begins causing strange emotional disturbances among visitors, forcing the PCs to trace its origin.

npc_seeds.name: Rico Valdez, Underground Impresario
npc_seeds.summary: A neonate Toreador who runs a semi-legal music venue and art space in a bad part of town, championing mortal and Anarch creatives.
npc_seeds.role: subculture hub leader and Anarch-adjacent contact
npc_seeds.temperament: electric, impulsive, loyal to his scene before any sect
possible_plots: Rico’s venue becomes a hotbed of Masquerade risk as more Kindred use it to feed; he begs the PCs for help enforcing rules.
An elder Toreador is trying to buy out and sanitize the space; Rico wants the coterie to help him resist without getting crushed.
A band playing at his club accidentally channels something supernatural in a performance, drawing interest from otherworldly forces.

npc_seeds.name: Lucille d’Aubigny, Fallen Star
npc_seeds.summary: Once a famous ballerina, now an elder Toreador who cannot accept that her body and the world have moved on.
npc_seeds.temperament: melancholic, gracious, viciously spiteful when reminded of time’s passage
possible_plots: Lucille fixates on a mortal dancer as her 'second chance' and pressures the PCs to eliminate any obstacles in the dancer’s career.
She offers rare information or artifacts in exchange for staging an elaborate performance that re-creates her greatest triumph.
Her haven is filled with eerie, frozen tableaux of past productions, some of which are more than mere props.

npc_seeds.name: Mika, Digital Visionary
npc_seeds.summary: A Toreador who abandoned traditional media for VR, AR, and immersive installations, treating the city itself as a canvas.
npc_seeds.temperament: hyper, distracted, intensely curious about other supernaturals
possible_plots: Mika’s latest AR project inadvertently reveals glimpses of real Kindred auras, drawing the attention of the Sheriff.
They blackmail the coterie with subtly edited footage unless the PCs help them secure rare tech hardware or occult data.
Mika offers to create a 'myth' around the PCs in the mortal web, improving their influence but making anonymity impossible.

npc_seeds.name: Father Gabriel Hayes, Gothic Priest
npc_seeds.summary: A Toreador who embraced the aesthetics of faith rather than the doctrine, serving as a priest in an almost purely aesthetic sense.
npc_seeds.role: confessor, manipulator of guilt and hope
npc_seeds.temperament: soft-spoken, theatrically humble, attuned to the sins people want to confess
possible_plots: Gabriel becomes the unofficial confessor for several city elders, and the PCs must decide whether to trade in secrets obtained through him.
He commissions a morbid religious artwork that troubles even other Kindred; its creation requires morally questionable components.
A mortal congregation around his church begins to develop fanatical devotion that may indicate outside supernatural influence.

sections.title: Opening Fiction
sections.notes: Sets the emotional tone of Toreador existence through a focused story of obsession, beauty, and decay.

sections.title: Clan History
sections.notes: Outlines Toreador myths of origin and their role in mortal cultural evolution from antiquity to the modern night.

sections.title: Culture and Practices
sections.notes: Describes Toreador social customs, salons, artistic fixations, and internal expectations of taste and comportment.

sections.title: Philosophy and Worldview
sections.notes: Explores how Toreador justify their obsession with beauty and their role as curators of meaning in an eternal night.

sections.title: Factions and Politics
sections.notes: Covers subgroups within the clan and how they leverage art, reputation, and Elysium to influence Camarilla power structures.

sections.title: Relations With Other Clans
sections.notes: Summarizes Toreador attitudes toward other clans and how those clans stereotype or exploit Toreador.

sections.title: Character Creation Notes
sections.notes: Provides guidance on building Toreador characters, including concept suggestions and mechanical emphases.

sections.title: Archetypes and Templates
sections.notes: Lists prebuilt Toreador concepts and example characters illustrating common clan roles.

sections.title: Storyteller Advice
sections.notes: Offers direction for using Toreador in chronicles, including how to center stories around art, reputation, and emotional stakes.
